topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumer rights in various sectors including food safety, telecommunications, and disability services.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem the arsenic compound known as roxarsone to be an unsafe food additive.
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to consider regulation of inmate telephone service.
Document 3: Requires states to obtain authorization from individuals with developmental disabilities before pursuing legal remedies on their behalf.

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Business Growth
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing tax benefits and credits to promote investment in renewable energy, employee ownership plans, and small business research initiatives.

Document 1: allow a 30% energy tax credit for equipment used to produce electricity by geothermal power
allow an extension of the grant period for specified energy property
Document 2: exempt certain distributions by S corporations to an employee stock ownership plan from the penalty tax
allow deferral of the recognition of gain for certain sales to ESOPs
Document 3: allow a qualified small business to elect to use a portion of its tax credit for increasing research expenditures as an offset against its payroll tax liability
limits the number of years a taxpayer may elect to offset payroll taxes under this Act to five

topic.name: Educational Policy and Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving educational standards, funding, and safety in schools and higher education institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to award formula matching grants to states or partnerships between states and experienced nonprofit providers of financial literacy or personal finance education programs.
Requires grantees to use 25% of the grant funds to develop financial literacy standards and assessments for at least three grade levels.
Document 2: Requires states and local educational agencies (LEAs) to obtain an FBI background check on individuals prior to offering them employment as school employees.
Withholds funds available to states for planning and administration and to LEAs as subgrants under part A of title II if they violate such prohibitions or fail to take such actions.
Document 3: This bill amends title I (General Provisions) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify consumer information disclosure requirements related to college costs and student characteristics.
The bill directs ED to develop, annually update, and make publicly available a College Dashboard website to replace the College Navigator website.

topic.name: Health Care Policy Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at improving health care access and reimbursement under Medicare and Medicaid programs.

Document 1: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
increase the Medicare reimbursement rate for clinical social worker services
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to calculate the federal upper reimbursement limit
Document 3: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare)
provides for continuity of coverage of prescription drugs under Medicare prescription drug plans

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Measures
topic.description: Legislative actions related to the temporary suspension of duties on specific goods and materials in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain non-women's footwear.
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Chloroacetic acid, sodium salt.
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain manufacturing equipment.
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the health care and benefits for veterans, particularly addressing specific medical needs and formularies.

Document 1: Caring for Veterans with Traumatic Brain Injury Act of 2007
Committee on Care of Veterans with Traumatic Brain Injury to continually assess VHA capability
Document 2: Joint Formularies for Veterans Act of 2015
uniform formulary with respect to systemic pain and psychotropic drugs
Document 3: additional special monthly pension
permanent and total non-service-connected disability

topic.name: Violence and Victim Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing violence and protecting victims, particularly in domestic, military, and child contexts.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to prevent any individual convicted of a felony or crime of domestic violence in a foreign court from possessing a firearm in the United States.
Document 2: Establishes in the Department of Defense (DOD) an Office of the Victims' Advocate to facilitate access to services for victims of domestic or family violence, sexual assault, and stalking in the military.
Directs the Secretary of Defense...to provide for a victims' advocates program within each military department.
Document 3: Amends the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 to extend through FY2012 funding under such Act for...grants to combat sexual abuse of children.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating financial institutions and protecting consumers in the lending market.

Document 1: amends the Truth in Lending Act
create a safe harbor from lawsuit for creditors
Document 2: amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council to determine that a bank holding company shall be subject to enhanced supervision
Document 3: amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit payday loans
declares payday loan instruments or transactions void from inception

topic.name: Land Management and Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, development, and conservation of federal and tribal lands, including provisions for economic development and resource management.

Document 1: Extinguishes all claims and all right, title, and interest in and to specified Indian and non-Indian land
Establishes a tribal trust fund account for economic development and land acquisition.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to establish a visitor center and field office
Amends the Mesquite Lands Act to implement a multispecies habitat conservation plan.
Document 3: Directs the Department of the Interior to convey federal land to La Paz County
Permits the county to retain conveyed federal land for public recreation and public purposes.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws and amendments related to the regulation of campaign contributions, expenditures, and communication in political campaigns.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to treat as a campaign contribution any payment made by any person for a coordinated expenditure.
Defines 'covered communication' as a public communication which promotes or supports the candidate, or attacks or opposes an opponent of the candidate.
Document 2: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions to prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls.

Defines a 'politically-oriented recorded message telephone call' as an outbound telephone call that plays a recorded message the purpose of which is to promote, advertise, campaign, or solicit donations.
Document 3: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more.
Any amount transferred by a joint fundraising committee established by a candidate's authorized committee to any other authorized committee of that candidate shall be treated as a contribution.

topic.name: Immigration and Labor Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to immigration, specifically concerning temporary and permanent residency for workers and their families in the context of labor needs in the United States.

Document 1: Establish an H-2C nonimmigrant visa for agricultural labor
Requires an employer to file an H-2C petition with the Department of Agriculture
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to adjust the status of an eligible alien entrepreneur
Requires that an eligible alien is present in the United States on the date of the application's filing
Document 3: Prohibit the Secretary of Homeland Security from approving family-based immigration petitions by those owing child support arrearages
Authorize the Secretary to revoke a previously-approved petition if certain conditions are met

topic.name: Military and Intelligence Funding
topic.description: Legislative documents related to the authorization and appropriation of funds for military and intelligence activities.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Defense to transfer members of the Armed Forces receiving outpatient care to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
Terminates the transfer requirement after certification from the Secretary of Defense.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2007 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
Allows the Director of Central Intelligence to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2007.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating drug approval processes, safety standards, and disposal practices for pharmaceuticals.

Document 1: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
prioritize the review of generic drug applications
Document 2: directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA)
study to examine the classification of ultraviolet tanning lamps
Document 3: amends the Controlled Substances Act
authorize an ultimate user or care taker to dispose of a controlled substance

topic.name: Mental Health and Substance Use Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving mental health and substance use services, including prevention, treatment, and support for at-risk populations.

Document 1: Reauthorizes and revises a grant program to enhance services for students with mental health or substance use disorders at institutions of higher education.
Expands the program's focus from youth suicides to suicides among all ages, particularly among groups that are at high risk for suicide.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish the Commission for Evidence-Based Mental and Substance Use Health Care.
Requires specified federal programs that provide mental health and substance use care to pay for peer support and illness self-management programs.
Document 3: Requires the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to design national strategies and implement programs to address chronic homelessness.

Priority for grants is given to applicants that target services to individuals and families who have experienced more homelessness, use more public emergency care, or have a history with the criminal justice system.

topic.name: School Nutrition and Food Programs
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving school food services and promoting healthy meal options for students.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to issue loan guarantees to local educational agencies to finance the construction, remodeling, or expansion of infrastructure or the purchase of durable equipment that will facilitate their provision of healthy meals.
Favors loan guarantee applicants that demonstrate a substantial or disproportionate need for food service infrastructure.
Document 2: Establishes a program for the procurement of biobased products to enhance sustainability in food services.
Provides grants to land-grant colleges and universities to develop renewable energy and value-added curricula.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to conduct a pilot program under which the Secretary will give participating states the option of receiving a grant to purchase fresh fruits and vegetables.
Grants shall be used to distribute fresh produce to schools participating in food service programs.

topic.name: Iran Sanctions and Nuclear Policy
topic.description: The topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures and sanctions related to Iran's nuclear program and economic activities.

Document 1: Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to expand the definitions of 'person' and 'petroleum resources'
Subjects Iran to certain economic sanctions.
Document 2: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to direct the President to transmit agreements regarding Iran's nuclear program to Congress
Ensures that Iran's permitted nuclear activities will not be used to further any nuclear-related military or nuclear explosive purpose.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should use diplomatic and economic measures to resolve the Iranian nuclear problem
Declares that nothing in this Act shall be construed to authorize the use force against Iran.

topic.name: Family and Child Welfare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving literacy, social security benefits, and pension transparency for families and children.

Document 1: authorizes grants to improve literacy and English skills for families
focuses on limited English proficient individuals who are parents or children
Document 2: entitles uninsured children to monthly benefits
amends Social Security Act to provide benefits for children
Document 3: requires reporting on pension benefit plans for public employees
ensures transparency in pension funding for families and employees

topic.name: Transportation Legislation and Security
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to public transportation funding, aviation security, and the operation of unmanned aircraft systems.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make emergency grants to states for public transportation projects.
Requires amounts apportioned to be used for operating expenses necessary to restore or prevent a reduction in public transportation service.
Document 2: Authorizes the Department of Homeland Security to establish U.S. Customs and Border Protection preclearance operations in a foreign country.
Ensures merchandise destined for the United States complies with applicable U.S. customs laws.
Document 3: Reauthorizes Federal Aviation Administration operations and specified FAA programs.
The FAA shall issue guidance for the operation of public drones.

topic.name: Fisheries Management and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at the management, conservation, and regulatory frameworks for fisheries in the United States, focusing on sustainable practices and regional governance.

Document 1: amends the Saltonstall-Kennedy Act to revise the program under which the Department of Commerce provides financial assistance for fisheries research and development projects
Commerce must carry out a national program of fisheries research and investment that supports rebuilding and maintaining healthy U.S. fish populations
Document 2: amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to revise requirements on fishing and fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico
State jurisdiction over fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico is expanded to nine nautical miles
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the South Atlantic red snapper fishery
Prohibits the Secretary from promulgating any interim rule... that prohibits fishing in the South Atlantic red snapper fishery before such report demonstrates the need for interim or final rules

topic.name: Labor Representation and Rights
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing employee representation and rights in the workplace through streamlined certification processes for labor organizations.

Document 1: Employee Free Choice Act of 2016
require the National Labor Relations Board to certify, without an election, an individual or labor organization to be the exclusive representative of the employees
Document 2: Workplace Democracy Act
require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to certify without an election an individual or labor organization as the exclusive representative of the employees

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation and Standards
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating telecommunications services and enhancing public safety communications standards.

Document 1: Cell Phone Bill Shock Act of 2011
requires the FCC to promulgate regulations for notifying subscribers about service usage limits.
Document 2: Public Safety Broadband Network Enabling Act
requires documentation of operational requirements for a nationwide interoperable public safety broadband network.
Document 3: Four Corners Television Access Act of 2009
amends federal copyright law to allow satellite or cable retransmission of broadcast signals.

topic.name: Data Protection and Security Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on the protection of personal information and the establishment of security protocols for data management across various sectors.

Document 1: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring commercial entities... to implement information security policies and procedures for the treatment and protection of personal information.
Establishes procedures to be followed in the event of an information security breach.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to promulgate regulations to require... each new light-, medium-, and heavy-duty motor vehicle manufactured for sale or lease in the United States to be equipped with an event data recorder (EDR) meeting certain requirements.

Prohibits the retrieval of information recorded or transmitted by an EDR by any person other than the owner of the motor vehicle... unless a court authorizes it, the owner consents, or the information is retrieved by a government motor vehicle safety agency.
Document 3: Defines 'personally identifiable information' as any information about an individual maintained by a federal agency... that can be used to distinguish or trace such individual's identity.
Includes within the information security duties of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB): reviewing agency information security programs...

topic.name: Conservation and Wildlife Management
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at conserving wildlife species and managing their habitats while balancing recreational and military activities.

Document 1: expands access to, and opportunities for, hunting, fishing, and recreational shooting
facilitate hunting, fishing, and recreational shooting on federal public land
Document 2: addresses the management of the greater sage-grouse
require states that opt to have plans in lieu of federal endangered species plans to monitor and report on relevant data
Document 3: establish Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas for national defense purposes
conserving the southern sea otter in such Areas while allowing reasonable military readiness activities

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the support and resources available to small businesses in the United States.

Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to exclude as a small business, for purposes of meeting federal agency contracting goals with small businesses, any small business that is publicly traded
Requires the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to notify the head of each federal department or agency regarding this Act and its amendments.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Actions
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative measures addressing specific designations, energy facility regulations, and tribal compensation settlements.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4122 Madison Street, Elfers, Florida, as the 'Private First Class Felton Roger Fussell Memorial Post Office.'
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to offer assistance to states to enable them to assign responsibilities delegated to them under federal law regarding coordinated and expeditious construction or expansion of a petroleum refining facility.
Designates DOE as the lead agency for coordinating applicable federal refinery authorizations and related environmental reviews with respect to a designated refinery.
Document 3: Establishes the Spokane Tribe of Indians Settlement Fund.
Requires the payment of compensation to the Spokane Business Council for the use of tribal lands for the generation of hydropower from the Grand Coulee Dam.

topic.name: Election and Voting Rights Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving election processes and restoring voting rights for various groups.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code and the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the system of public financing for presidential primary and general elections.
Limits contributions to presidential primary candidates who participate in the public financing system to $1,000 from individual contributors.
Document 2: Restores the right of District of Columbia residents to participate as Maryland residents in congressional elections and presidential elections.
Repeals provisions of the District of Columbia Delegate Act that establishes the office of District of Columbia Delegate to the House of Representatives.
Document 3: Amends the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 (NVRA) to direct states to ensure the availability of the Internet for online voter registration.
Requires states to consider an applicant to have provided a valid voter registration form if he or she has provided all necessary information to demonstrate eligibility to vote.

topic.name: U.S. Trade Enforcement Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the enforcement of trade laws and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Tariff Act of 1930 with respect to the administration and enforcement of antidumping (AD) and countervailing duty (CVD) orders
the USITC must evaluate in examining the impact of imports of merchandise on a domestic industry in material injury determinations.
Document 2: amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to bar revocation of a determination by the administering authority that the People's Republic of China is a nonmarket economy country
the administering authority determines and reports to Congress that China no longer meets the requirements of a nonmarket economy country.

topic.name: Access to Education and Technology
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving access to educational resources and technology for underserved populations, particularly in early childhood education and minority-serving institutions.

Document 1: expand access to high-quality child care for infants and toddlers from low-income families
increase parental options for and access to such care
Document 2: award grants, contracts, or cooperative agreements to eligible institutions to provide educational instruction in digital and wireless network technologies
strengthen the national digital and wireless infrastructure
Document 3: make competitive grants to eligible institutions for specified activities
providing technology-related training for teachers, library and media specialists, early childhood educators

topic.name: Unborn Child Pain Awareness and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on informing women about potential pain to unborn children during abortions and regulating the timing and circumstances under which abortions can be performed.

Document 1: require an abortion provider who knowingly performs an abortion of a pain-capable unborn child to first inform the woman of the probable age of the child
there is substantial evidence that the process of being killed in an abortion will cause the unborn child pain
Document 2: prohibit any person from performing or attempting to perform an abortion except in conformity with this Act's requirements
the probable post-fertilization age of the unborn child is 20 weeks or greater, except where necessary to save the life of a pregnant woman
Document 3: require an abortion provider who knowingly performs an abortion of a pain-capable unborn child to first inform the woman of the probable age of the child
creates an exception to save the life of the mother

topic.name: Federal Legislative Actions
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving federal management, addressing trade issues, and supporting specific initiatives.

Document 1: Savings, Accountability, Value, and Efficiency Act of 2015 or the SAVE Act
Requires the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to publish a national strategy for managing excess and underutilized federal real property.
Document 2: Unfair Chinese Automotive Tariff Equalization Act - Prohibits the importation into the United States of any motor vehicle that is the product of the People's Republic of China (PRC).
Document 3: Peace Stamp for the Peace Corps Act - Directs the U.S. Postal Service to issue a semipostal to benefit the Peace Corps.

topic.name: Coal Mining and Environmental Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to coal mining practices, environmental protection, and land management in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing or approving any proposed or final regulation under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act that would adversely impact employment in coal mines.
Reduce the quantity of coal available for domestic consumption or for export.
Document 2: Amends the Solid Waste Disposal Act to authorize states to adopt and implement coal combustion residuals permit programs.
Establishes minimum requirements for CCRs permit programs.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to convey federal land within the Coconino National Forest.
Revokes public orders withdrawing any of the federal land from appropriation or disposal under the public land laws.

topic.name: Commemorative Legislation and Recognition
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to the commemoration of significant historical events or organizations and the recognition of Indian tribes.

Document 1: Mall Designation and Revitalization Act authorizes modifications for commemorative works in the National Mall.
Requires recommendations for enhancing the selection and design process of commemorative works.
Document 2: Boy Scouts of America Centennial Commemorative Coin Act directs minting of coins to commemorate the centennial of the Boy Scouts of America.
Subjects coin sales to a surcharge for funding local councils in hard-to-serve areas.
Document 3: Indian Tribal Federal Recognition Administrative Procedures Act establishes a process for Indian groups to gain federal recognition.
Requires evidence of descent from historical Indian groups and distinct community status since 1900.

topic.name: Regulatory Compliance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on establishing compliance requirements and penalties for various sectors including foreign investment, child labor, and anti-money laundering.

Document 1: Permits a foreign person... to acquire ownership... subject to reciprocity requirements.
Establishes civil penalties for violations of this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act... to repeal certain exemptions from child labor prohibitions.
Increases civil and establishes criminal penalties for child labor violations.
Document 3: Revises certain requirements for anti-money laundering programs...
Sets forth civil and criminal penalties for violations of this Act.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments for Public Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments aimed at addressing public policy issues such as compensation limits, unemployment benefits, and surveillance regulations.

Document 1: Excessive Pay Shareholder Approval Act - Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to prohibit the compensation for an employee of an issuer of securities in any single taxable year from exceeding 100 times the average compensation for services performed by all the issuer's employees during such taxable year.
Allows higher compensation only if at least 60% of the shareholders have voted to approve it.
Document 2: FAA Sunsets Extension Act of 2012 - Amends the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend until June 1, 2015: (1) the date for repeal of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978.
Requires compliance reviews of surveillance targeting and minimization procedures to be provided to the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community.
Document 3: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2013 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before January 1, 2015.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until December 31, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.

topic.name: U.S. Government Structure and Responsibilities
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of the U.S. government's structure, including appointments, legislative powers, and property management.

Document 1: the President shall appoint, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, an individual to serve as the Director of the American Institute in Taiwan.
Document 2: the legislative power of the District shall extend to all rightful subjects of legislation within the District consistent with the U.S. Constitution
Establishes a Council of the District of Columbia and the Office of the Mayor.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Navy to transfer the decommissioned Naval Security Group Activity
Requires the Secretary of the Navy to submit reports on the progress made in connection with the remediation and transfer of the property.

topic.name: U.S. Border and Veteran Assistance Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to border security, drug trafficking prevention, and assistance for veterans and individuals affected by disasters in the Philippines.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) and the Attorney General to provide to a state on a U.S. border up to 500 additional U.S. Border Patrol agents, Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) agents, and Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) agents to patrol and defend the border.

Establishes the ATF, DEA, and Border Patrol Task Force to monitor and report to Congress and the President on the trade and sale of drugs, alcohol, tobacco, firearms, and explosives along U.S. borders.
Document 2: Authorizes the President to provide assistance to the Republic of the Philippines to provide benefits to persons who served in the organized military forces of the government of the Commonwealth of the Philippines.
Includes within the benefits to be provided: compensation for service-connected disabilities; medical care; education benefits; and death pensions.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the extraordinary and temporary conditions caused by flooding and other catastrophic damage wrought by Typhoon Haiyan in the Philippines qualifies the Philippines for designation under section 244 of the Immigration and Nationality Act.
Provides 18-month temporary protected status for a national of the Philippines who has been continuously physically present in the United States since November 8, 2013.

topic.name: School Safety and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving school environments through positive behavioral interventions, safety measures, and health guidelines for students.

Document 1: create a positive school climate for all students
improve engagement for disconnected youths
Document 3: establish concussion management guidelines that address the prevention, identification, treatment, and management of concussions in school-aged children

topic.name: Environmental Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing environmental issues and promoting public safety related to environmental hazards.

Document 1: Global Warming Education Act requires a program to broaden understanding of human induced global warming and promote renewable energy.
Emphasizes actionable information to help implement energy conservation and greenhouse gas reduction.
Document 2: Dangerous Products Warning Act imposes penalties for failing to warn about serious dangers associated with products or business practices.
Targets businesses and supervisors who knowingly fail to inform about dangers.
Document 3: Stockman Effect Act requires study on the impact of Earth's magnetic field shifts on weather.

topic.name: Water and Energy Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on the management and conservation of water and energy resources, including watershed programs and energy production initiatives.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a Cooperative Watershed Management Program
promoting the sustainable use of the water resources of the relevant watershed
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct offshore oil and gas Lease Sale 220
Establishes in the Treasury the Alternative Energy Trust Fund

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts that regulate and amend policies related to federal assistance, national insurance, and agricultural programs.

Document 1: amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961
may not receive assistance under the Act
Document 2: Establishes in the Department of the Treasury an Office of National Insurance
Requires the Commissioner to supervise, regulate, and provide for registration of insurance self-regulatory organizations
Document 3: amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
make the current producer payment formula permanent

topic.name: Land Conservation and Recreation Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on land conservation, management of recreational areas, and environmental protection in relation to public lands.

Document 1: Withdraws specified National Forest System land from all forms of appropriation or disposal
Makes the Bridger-Teton National Land and Resource Management Plan applicable to the withdrawal area.
Document 2: Establishes the Clear Creek National Recreation Area to promote environmentally responsible off-highway vehicle recreation
Interior shall create a comprehensive management plan for the Recreation Area.

topic.name: Health and Industry Innovation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at promoting innovation, competition, and access to services in health insurance and the textile industry.

Document 1: Amends the McCarran-Ferguson Act to provide that nothing in that Act shall modify, impair, or supersede the operation of any of the antitrust laws with respect to the business of health insurance.
Applies prohibitions against using unfair methods of competition to the business of health insurance.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a grant program to fund textile research and innovation in the U.S. textile and fiber products industry.
Gives preference in the award of such grants to collaborative research organizations that emphasize peer-reviewed research by leading academic and industry experts.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to provide a dental insurance plan for any veteran enrolled in the Department of Veterans Affairs annual patient enrollment system.
Provides for the payment of premiums for plan enrollment.

topic.name: Veterans and Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures related to veterans' education benefits and health care regulations.

Document 1: Post-9/11 Veterans Education Fairness Act allows transfer of educational benefits to family members.
Entitlement transfer is restricted to service members.
Document 2: Economic Recovery in Health Care Act prohibits finalizing certain health care administrative actions until April 1, 2009.
Amends previous appropriations acts related to veterans' care and health services.
Document 3: Unsafe Meat and Poultry Recall Act amends federal food safety laws related to public health risks.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to manage recalls of adulterated or misbranded products.

topic.name: Human Rights Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting and promoting the rights of individuals, both domestically and internationally.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Service Act of 1980 to include in the Foreign Service professional development program training to protect the rights of U.S. citizens in foreign custody.

Prohibits foreign assistance from being provided to an agency or other entity of a foreign government that is responsible for the violation of, or is failing to protect, the fundamental due process or human rights of an imprisoned U.S. citizen.
Document 2: Declares that it is an unlawful employment practice for employers to fail to make reasonable accommodations to known limitations related to pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions.
Sets forth enforcement procedures and remedies under the Civil Rights Act of 1964.

topic.name: Consultation and Legal Protections
topic.description: The documents focus on legal frameworks and protections regarding consultation processes with tribes, witness protection, and regulations on indecent advertising.

Document 1: consultation with Indian tribes involves more than simply notifying a tribe
ensure meaningful and timely input by Indian tribes before undertaking any activity
Document 2: impose criminal penalties on any person who kills, or attempts to kill, a witness
prevent the production of a record or document in a state or local judicial proceeding
Document 3: revise the FCC's interpretations of, and enforcement policies concerning, FCC regulations on indecent material
treat as indecent any advertisement for medications treating erectile dysfunction

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of significant U.S. legislative acts addressing various national issues including security, energy, and foreign policy.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to revise the prohibition against entering restricted federal buildings or grounds
Defines 'restricted buildings or grounds' as a posted, cordoned off, or otherwise restricted area
Document 2: Sets forth provisions concerning clean energy, energy efficiency, reducing global warming pollution
Establishing a cap-and-trade system for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions
Document 3: Declares that it is U.S. policy to end the occupation of Iraq
Requires the withdrawal from Iraq of all U.S. Armed Forces and contractor security forces within six months

topic.name: Legislative Proposals and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative proposals aimed at addressing specific issues such as harassment, veterans' benefits, and water management.

Document 1: Preventing Harassment through Outbound Number Enforcement Act of 2009
impose a fine, a five-year prison term, or both, on anyone who knowingly uses or provides to another, in interstate or foreign commerce, false caller ID information
Document 2: Revises provisions concerning the post-9/11 veterans' educational assistance program
qualify for program assistance full-time service in the National Guard
Document 3: San Luis Unit Drainage Resolution Act
requires the Department of the Interior to implement the Agreement between the United States and Westlands Water District

topic.name: Ocean and Marine Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the exploration, management, and preservation of oceanic, marine, and coastal resources.

Document 1: establish within NOAA a coordinated national ocean exploration program
increase scientific knowledge essential for the informed management, use, and preservation of oceanic, marine, and coastal areas
Document 2: exempt from the harbor maintenance tax cargo contained in intermodal cargo containers
Great Lakes Saint Lawrence Seaway System

topic.name: Government Oversight and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to government accountability, security services, and emergency response systems in the context of national security and disaster management.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State to ensure that only government personnel provide security services at U.S. diplomatic or consular missions in Iraq.
Requires certain reports to Congress regarding such contracts.
Document 2: Revises certain administrative requirements for the Government Accountability Office (GAO).
Requires the Comptroller General's annual report to Congress to assess the overall degree of federal agency cooperation with GAO audits.
Document 3: Establishes within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) a Metropolitan Medical Response System Program to support state and local jurisdictions in preparing for and maintaining all-hazards response capabilities.
Authorizes the use of contracts to support the integration of emergency management, health, and medical systems into a coordinated response to mass casualty incidents.

topic.name: Regulatory Frameworks for Industry
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating and promoting specific industries, including concrete masonry, employment verification, and marine resource management.

Document 1: Concrete Masonry Products Research, Education, and Promotion Act of 2012
establishment of a Concrete Masonry Products Board
Document 2: Improving Methods to Promote Regular Occurrences of the Verification of Employability Status Act of 2008
amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act
Document 3: National Sea Grant College Program Amendments Act of 2008
adds regional and national projects as elements of the national sea grant college program
